content: Self-Refine's iterative single-model feedback loop can be grounded by multi-perspective LLM self-reflection mechanisms to solve the key flaw of blind self-correction when no external supervision is available. By injecting inconsistent, alternative solving perspectives from LLM self-reflection frameworks like Self-Contrast into each iteration of Self-Refine, we can create a self-supervised refinement pipeline that retains Self-Refine's core advantage of no extra training or labeled data while reducing the risk of the model reinforcing its own incorrect biases.
